perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information, and any Derivative of it, whether created by the AOC or Company, remains the property of the AOC and no license or other rights to Confidential Information is gr anted to Company. For the purpose of this Agreement, “Derivative ” means: (i) for copyrightable or copyrighted material, any translation, abridgment, revision or other form in which an existing work may be recast, transform ed or adapted, (ii) for patentable or patented material, any improvement or enhanc ement of it; and (iii) for material which is protected by trade secret, any new material derived from existing trade secret material, incl uding new material which may be protected by any of the following: copyrigh t, patent or trade secret.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Data, and any Derivative of it, whether created by the AOC or Company, remains the property of the AOC and no license or other rights to Confidential Information is gr anted to Company. For the purpose of this Agreement, “Derivative ” means: (i) for copyrightable or copyrighted material, any translation, abridgment, revision or other form in which an existing work may be recast, transform ed or adapted, (ii) for patentable or patented material, any improvement or enhanc ement of it; and (iii) for material which is protected by trade secret, any new material derived from existing trade secret material, incl uding new material which may be protected by any of the following: copyrigh t, patent or trade secret.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in Section 4, while maintaining the original definition related to 'Confidential Information' creates ambiguity. The contract refers to 'Confidential Data' as subject to ownership while the definition of derivative is tied to the original term, creating confusion over ownership and rights management, and will likely create confusion with the definition of 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidentia l Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' introduces a strong contradiction because the definition of 'Derivative' remains tied to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text specifies the return of documents containing 'Confidential Information.' If 'Confidential Data' is now distinct from 'Confidential Information,' it creates uncertainty as to whether documents containing only 'Confidential Data' (and not 'Confidential Information') must be returned. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation where using terms interchangeably without definition leads to conflicting obligations, creating ambiguity about the obligations of the company regarding the return of information. The changed text now creates a scenario where the return of 'Confidential Data' might not be explicitly required by the contradicted section, depending on whether it's considered 'Confidential Information' or not. This creates a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidentia l Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 4 of 5 use of the Confiden tial Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Sensitive Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all Attachment 8 Non-Disclosure Agreement for Request for Proposal – California Courts Protective Order Registry Development Page 4 of 5 use of the Confiden tial Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in Section 5, without defining 'Sensitive Information', introduces a critical contradiction. Section 5 now stipulates the return of 'Sensitive Information' while the usage is disconjointed because it also makes use of 'Confidential Information', whose meaning is unclear. This contradiction complicates the return obligations, as it becomes unclear what specific information the Company is obligated to return, especially given the lack of a clear definition for Sensitive information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE AOC MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, CO MPLETENESS, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in the return obligation section, while the contradicted text refers only to 'Confidential Information' and its lack of warranties. Because 'Sensitive Information' isn't defined, the obligation to return it is unclear and the lack of warranty does not apply to it, creating a contradiction because it's unclear which information the company is obligated to return or not return and further makes the meaning of the 'Confidential Information' warranty clause unclear.

perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE AOC MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, CO MPLETENESS, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL Protected Data IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE AOC MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, CO MPLETENESS, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.explanation: The substitution of "Confidential Information" for "Protected Data" in Section 8 creates a direct contradiction by leaving "Confidential Information" ambiguous in the text. It is unclear whether the disclaimers and waivers of warranties apply equally to Protected Data as to what was previously termed 'Confidential Information'. This undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement because of what this change entails.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage. In such event, the party alleging breach of this Agreement shall be entitled, as a matter of right, without further notice, to require of the other party specific performance of all of the acts and undertakings required of the other party hereunder and to obtain injunctive and other equitable relief in any competent court to prevent the violation or threatened violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party. Neither this provision nor any exer cise by either party of its rights to equitable relief or specific performance herein granted shall constitute a waiver by either party of any other rights which it may have to, damages or otherwise. If either party brings su it to enforce the terms hereof, the successful party in such suit shall be ent itled to receive all of its reasonable costs of litigation, including attorneys' fees.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Protected Data" creates ambiguity because the contradicted text refers specifically to "Confidential Information." As the explanation states, it is unclear whether the waivers apply to what was previously termed 'Confidential Information', undermining the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement. This represents a misaligned terminology issue that directly contradicts the section that assumes 'Confidential Information' maintains a unique character and is protected, potentially causing irreparable harm if breached. Because the agreement no longer clearly defines what Confidential Information means it is ambiguous and therefore contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Sensitive Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in Section 9, where damages are discussed, creates confusion because the agreement continues to refer to Confidential Information in the clauses discussing breach and enforcement of the agreement. Without a definition for Sensitive Information, it's unclear whether a breach related to Sensitive Information warrants the remedies described, thus this introduction of Sensitive Information and continuing to make use of Confidential Information in the text, is going to create contradictory actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall survive until such time as all Confid ential Information disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Company. Except as otherwise provided in any future agreement, either party may terminate this Agreement by written notice to the other. Not withstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in the Changed Text, while the Contradicted Text continues to refer to 'Confidential Information', creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces a new term without proper definition or replacement of the old term. The Contradicted Text outlines obligations and rights related to 'Confidential Information'. Because the Changed Text uses 'Sensitive Information' and no definition of Sensitive Information is given, or it is not stated that Sensitive Information = Confidential Information, a reasonable reader could interpret that the obligations in the Contradicted Text regarding Confidential Information do not apply to Sensitive Information. This creates a strong and obvious contradiction that would lead to confusion. Therefore, according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology, a contradiction exists.
